lines.speaker: Moggy
lines.text: Why, what brings you to my house, young [man/lady]?

lines.speaker: Moggy
lines.text: Me? Well, let's see... I live here. So I suppose I'm your host, as long as you're in my house.

lines.speaker: Moggy
lines.text: I used to work down at the beach, running the charcoal furnaces, but I've handed all that business to my son now. He's a good boy.

lines.speaker: Moggy
lines.text: Anyway, retiring isn't too bad. There's plenty of warmth from the lava pits, and my boy helps me carry my shopping from Munty's shop. I'll be alright.

lines.speaker: Moggy
lines.text: But you've never told me what you're doing in my house, young [man/lady]. Don't think I've forgotten!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you got anything to trade?

lines.speaker: Moggy
lines.text: Oh, are you a door-to-door [salesman/saleswoman]? My boy said I shouldn't let people like you into the house, and to call the guards if you bothered me.

lines.speaker: Moggy
lines.text: So why are you in my house anyway?

lines.speaker: Moggy
lines.text: Be a dear and just wander off now.
